_note: All banter copy. Use {PLAYER}, {TEAM}, {TEAM_A}, {TEAM_B}, {FRIEND}, {PTS}, {RANK}, {TIME}, {ANSWER}, {RUNS}, {N} as template vars. Arrays rotate randomly. Lines with {FRIEND} are auto-skipped when no friend name is available — so every key must have at least 2–3 lines without {FRIEND}.

open: {TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Card's open. Some of this group have already locked. You haven't.
Today's picks. {TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Don't be the last one in again.
{FRIEND} locked in. The card is still open. For you.
The card is open. {TEAM_A} or {TEAM_B}. You know what you think. Put it on the record.
Still haven't locked? {FRIEND} already has a take. Terrible take, but it's in.
{TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Everyone in this group has an opinion. Some of them are filing it officially.
Card's open. Clock's running. {FRIEND} is already done and judging.
saved: Picks saved. The card isn't locked until the match starts. Come back and change it 4 times if you need to.
In. You can still change this. The first ball is the deadline, not this.
Saved. Still editable. The match hasn't started. {FRIEND} is also still editing, for what it's worth.
Noted. Nobody's locking you in yet. That happens at the first ball.
Picks are in. Not final yet. The group is still watching.
locked: You're on record. Whatever happens next, you called it.
No take-backs. The match has started and so has the reckoning.
Card closed. The group is watching. {FRIEND}'s on the opposite side and very confident.
On the record. The match will settle this.
Locked. {FRIEND}'s in too. One of you is having a much worse night than the other.
The match has started. Your picks are final. Good luck. You'll need it.
missed: You had until the first ball. You didn't make it. {FRIEND} did. This will come up.
{TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B} started without your picks. The group has noticed and said nothing. Yet.
Zero picks. {FRIEND} submitted hours ago. The difference in commitment is being quietly documented.
Missed it. No points. No card. The group is already three messages into a conversation you're not in.
{TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B} started. You were not on the board. Everyone else was.
countdown: Lock in before {TIME} is gone. {FRIEND} is already done.
{TIME} on the clock. Make the call.

matchWinner: {TEAM} to win. On record. If they lose, this screenshot will surface immediately.
You picked {TEAM}. {FRIEND} picked the other side. One of you is going to have a terrible evening.
Backing {TEAM}. The {TEAM} fans in this group are unbearable when they win. Hope you're ready for that.
Going against your own team? Either you're being strategic or you've actually given up on them.
{TEAM}. Locked. The match will decide if this was brave or delusional.
{TEAM} to win. The group has been informed. The scoreboard gets the final vote.
theCall: {ANSWER}. The Call is set. The match will cooperate or it won't.
{ANSWER}. {FRIEND} went the other way. May the better guess win.
Locked: {ANSWER}. {FRIEND} is already second-guessing you and hasn't even said anything yet.
{ANSWER} — bold or obvious, the match will tell us which.
The Call: {ANSWER}. Committed. The scoreboard takes it from here.
villainPick: Villain locked: {PLAYER}. {FRIEND} picked someone different. One of you is a genius. Neither knows which.
You villain-picked {PLAYER}. Under 10 and you're laughing. Over 30 and you deserve everything that follows.
{PLAYER}. Your villain. {FRIEND} has recorded this. It will surface at exactly the wrong moment.
{PLAYER} is the villain. The match decides if you were clever or just optimistic about someone else's failure.
Villain: {PLAYER}. Ruthless pick. The cricket will tell us if it was warranted.
chaosBall: {ANSWER} on the Chaos Ball. {FRIEND} went the other way. The universe will settle this, not either of you.
Chaos Ball: {ANSWER}. This is either intuition or completely random. Indistinguishable, honestly.
{ANSWER}. The chaos has been acknowledged. It will do what it wants regardless.
Chaos Ball locked: {ANSWER}. May it cooperate. It probably won't.
{ANSWER} on Chaos Ball. {FRIEND} also said {ANSWER}. One of you is definitely guessing.
noSquad: Squad not announced yet — villain pick skipped for this match.
No squad data yet. Villain pick won't count this one.
Squads aren't in. This pick is sitting out.

justYou: You're the first one in. Now sit with the uncertainty for a bit.
First in. The group hasn't moved yet. Very on-brand for all of them.
Only your card right now. The others will eventually show up. Probably.
You're the only one locked in. Classic. Carry the group.

matchWinnerCorrect: {TEAM} won. You had it. +{PTS}. Say nothing and let the points speak.
Correct. {TEAM} came through. {PTS} points and the right to be smug without saying anything — which is somehow worse.
matchWinnerWrong: Wrong team. The scoreboard was not subtle about it.
{TEAM} lost. You predicted {TEAM} to win. These are the facts. The group has the facts.
Backed {TEAM}. {TEAM} had other plans. The plans did not include your prediction.
Zero on the winner. Nobody is saying anything. That silence is doing a lot of work.
{TEAM} didn't show up. Your prediction did not survive contact with the actual match.
theCallCorrect: {ANSWER} — and it happened. {PTS} points. Act surprised.
Right call. {ANSWER}. {PTS} pts. The group has been informed, whether they like it or not.
The Call: correct. +{PTS}. File that away for the next time someone questions your cricket sense.
theCallWrong: The Call missed. Someone in the group got it right. They have a tab for this.
Didn't land. {ANSWER} was wrong. The match made that very clear.
{ANSWER} didn't happen. The match had other ideas. Zero.
The Call missed. The conversation has already started. You're not going to enjoy it.
villainUnder10: Villain pick landed. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. Under 10. +15 pts. Ruthless and correct.
{PLAYER}: {RUNS} runs. Flopped exactly as called. +15. The villain narrative is running perfectly.
Under 10. {PLAYER} delivered the collapse. +15 pts. The accuracy is genuinely concerning.
Called it. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. Under 10. +15. Take a bow and try not to make it weird.
villainOver30: {PLAYER} scored {RUNS}. That's 30+. −5. The villain picked up the bat and completely ruined it.
Villain backfired. {PLAYER} got {RUNS}. The hero arc destroyed the villain pick. −5. Painful.
{PLAYER} had {RUNS}. The villain won. This is the wrong outcome in every possible sense. −5.
Should have picked someone who actually flops. {PLAYER}: {RUNS}. −5. The cricket disagreed with you entirely.
villainNeutral: {PLAYER} scored {RUNS}. Not under 10. Not 30+. The villain just sort of existed. Zero points, zero drama, zero satisfaction.
Grey zone. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. Not few enough to flop, not enough to destroy. Unsatisfying for everyone involved.
{PLAYER}: {RUNS}. Right in the middle. The match refused to commit either way. Classic.
Neutral. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. The villain had a mediocre match and so did this pick.
chaosBallCorrect: Chaos Ball landed. +{PTS}. Nobody planned for this. You sort of did.
Called it. Chaos Ball correct. +{PTS}. The chaos cooperated exactly once and you caught it.
The chaos cooperated. {PTS} points. Write this down. It won't happen like this again.
Chaos Ball: right. +{PTS}. The match delivered. Enjoy it, these don't come often.
chaosBallWrong: Chaos Ball missed. 0 pts. It was chaos. That's the entire point.
Didn't happen. Zero from the Chaos Ball. The chaos went exactly the other way.
Chaos Ball: wrong. The match had completely different ideas. It always has different ideas.
The chaos went the wrong way. Zero. It does this.

noCardYet: Nobody's filled their card yet. {TIME} left. Classic group energy.
Zero cards submitted. The group is thinking about it. {TIME} left to be first.
Still waiting on everyone. {TIME} left. Usual suspects, usual timeline.
Empty. The whole group is overthinking this. {TIME} on the clock.
noMatchesToday: Rest day. Revisit yesterday's damage or argue about it. Both will happen.
Nothing on today. The IPL is taking a day off. Use the time to prepare your excuses.
No fixture today. Come back tomorrow. The cricket will resume and so will the suffering.
Day off. {FRIEND} is already watching old highlights and making predictions about the past.
seasonPicksEmpty: Season picks aren't locked yet. Opens before the first match.
Fill your season picks before the tournament begins. {FRIEND} already has a draft.
Season predictions: 0 of {GROUP_SIZE} submitted. Nobody has committed to anything yet.

header: Full time. The numbers are in.
Match over. Whatever happened, happened. Here it is.
The cricket is done. The reckoning starts now.
groupSplitUnanimousCorrect: All {TOTAL} of you picked {TEAM}. All correct. No bragging rights when the whole group got it. You agreed, you got lucky, move on.
Unanimous. {TEAM} to win. Everyone right. The group chat will be suspiciously quiet about this one — can't claim it when everyone called the same thing.
groupSplitUnanimousWrong: All {TOTAL} of you picked {TEAM}. All wrong. Collective humiliation — which is somehow worse than just being wrong alone.
Unanimous. {TEAM} to win. {TEAM} didn't win. The group managed to be wrong in perfect agreement. Impressive in the worst way.
groupSplitEven: Dead split. {WINNER_COUNT} right, {LOSER_COUNT} wrong. Half the group celebrating, the other half composing very measured messages.
{WINNER_COUNT} called {TEAM}. {LOSER_COUNT} didn't. The group is now two groups and they are not on speaking terms.
groupSplitLoneCorrect: One person picked {TEAM}. That person was right. They have been insufferable for the last ten minutes and show no signs of stopping.
The lone {TEAM} call: correct. {WINNER_COUNT} of {TOTAL}. They said nothing before the match. They are making up for it now.
groupSplitLoneWrong: Everyone picked the winner except one. That one person is currently unavailable for comment.
{WINNER_COUNT} of {TOTAL} got it right. The holdout knows exactly what happened. The group is being polite. For now.
groupSplitMajorityWrong: {LOSER_COUNT} of you picked {TEAM}. {TEAM} lost. The {WINNER_COUNT} who got it right are being completely unbearable about it.
Majority call: {TEAM}. {TEAM} didn't show up. The minority was right. They said nothing before. They are making up for every second of that silence now.
groupSplit: {WINNER_COUNT} called {TEAM}. {LOSER_COUNT} didn't. The group was split.
The group was divided. {WINNER_COUNT} right, {LOSER_COUNT} wrong.
h2hWin: {FRIEND} takes the H2H. {OPPONENT} takes the loss and the silence that follows.
h2hLoss: {OPPONENT} won the H2H this match. They will mention this.
H2H: {OPPONENT} over {FRIEND}. The gap is noted.
H2H lost to {OPPONENT}. They're being very calm about it. That's the worst kind.

rankUp: Up {N} spots. {FRIEND} is watching the leaderboard and pretending not to.
#{RANK}. Moving. {FRIEND} dropped a place. Nobody has told them yet.
Climbed to #{RANK}. The board has updated. The group will notice.
Up {N} places. {FRIEND} has refreshed the leaderboard twice and is saying nothing.
rankDown: Down {N} places. {FRIEND} will bring this up at the worst possible time.
#{RANK}. Dropped. The group has been diplomatic so far. That will not last.
Down to #{RANK}. {FRIEND} is recalculating the gap. Not subtly.
#{RANK} now. Down {N}. It's early. That's what people say. It's not particularly true.
rankSame: Same spot. #{RANK}. {FRIEND} had a quiet match too. This isn't over.
#{RANK}. Still there. The table hasn't shifted yet.
No movement. #{RANK}. {FRIEND} is neither celebrating nor panicking. That might be the worst sign.
topOfLeaderboard: Top of the table. {FRIEND} is {N} pts back and not sleeping.
#1. The group is taking notes and pretending they aren't.
Leading. {FRIEND} has seen this and said nothing. Which is louder than something.
{FRIEND} leads. The others have circled this. It's early and nobody is treating it like it's early.

onboarding.welcome: Pick your team. It sets your colours for the season.
onboarding.teamPicked: Locked in as a {TEAM} fan. Bold choice.
onboarding.displayNamePrompt: What do the others call you?

seasonPicks.open: Season Picks are open. 7 calls. One shot. Nobody in this group is going to get all 7. Lock yours before Match 1.
seasonPicks.locked: Season Picks locked. 7 predictions in the vault. See you at the end of the tournament.
seasonPicks.partiallyFilled: You've filled {COUNT} of 7. The rest aren't going to pick themselves. {FRIEND} finished theirs already and is fully committed to all of them.
